steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is C, $x \left(19{x}^{2}+5x+8\right)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Find the greatest common factor
steps.content: Look at $19x^3+5x^2+8x$. The coefficients $19$, $5$, and $8$ share no common factor other than $1$. Every term has at least one factor of $x$, so the GCF is just $x$.

steps.title: Compare with each choice
steps.content:
• A) $19x(x^2+5x+8)$: distributes to $19x^3+95x^2+152x$, not the original. ✗
  • B) $8x(19x^3+5x^2+1)$: distributes to $152x^4+40x^3+8x$, which has an $x^4$ term. ✗
  • C) $x(19x^2+5x+8)$: distributes to $19x^3+5x^2+8x$. ✓
  • D) $32(x^3+x^2+x)$: distributes to $32x^3+32x^2+32x$, wrong coefficients. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
